pattern: Since the feasibility of keeping every run at or under some length only gets easier as that length grows, binary searching on the answer and, for each candidate length, computing the cheapest way to break the string into alternating blocks no longer than it turns an operations budget into a length.
hints: If a maximum run length is achievable with some number of flips, then any larger maximum run length is achievable with no more flips -- so binary search for the smallest length whose flip cost fits the budget.

For a fixed candidate maximum length L, the string must be split into consecutive blocks, each of size at most L, where each block is forced to a single bit and adjacent blocks must use opposite bits -- flipping every character in a block that disagrees with its assigned bit.

Track, for each possible ending bit, the cheapest way to reach that point using only blocks of length at most L; a sliding-window minimum over the last L starting points turns this into a linear scan instead of trying every possible block boundary.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, one flip splits a long run in two
statement example, two flips alternate the whole string
statement example, already alternating with zero budget
all identical characters with zero budget, run length stays the full string
a large budget that can fully alternate a long uniform string
already perfectly alternating, more budget than needed

stress.description: random binary strings with a random operations budget up to the length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 4000))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
    numOps = rng.randint(0, n)
    return [s, numOps]

stress.description: long runs of the same character punctuated by rare flips, with a modest budget
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 6000))
    s = []
    cur = rng.choice('01')
    while len(s) < n:
        run = rng.randint(5, 40)
        s.extend([cur] * run)
        cur = '1' if cur == '0' else '0'
    s = ''.join(s[:n])
    numOps = rng.randint(0, n // 10)
    return [s, numOps]
